methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $26M total raised proxy.

methods.rationale: 7 same-sector peers exist in Quantum Computing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Qedma is a Series A quantum computing company founded by Asif Sinay, Netanel Lindner, and Dorit Aharonov, having raised $26M+ to date. The quantum computing sector comprises 11 identified comparable companies: 3 public (IONQ, RGTI, QBTS) and 8 private peers including IonQ, Quantinuum, Rigetti Computing, D-Wave Quantum, and Infleqtion.

The stage-progression method is the applicable triangulation approach. Using total capital raised ($26M) as a valuation proxy and applying a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical for Series A-to-next-stage progression yields an implied valuation of $52M. The peer-median method could not be reliably applied due to insufficient narrow comparables at the same stage and valuation tier within quantum computing; while 7 same-sector peers exist, filtering by stage and similar tier yielded fewer than 3 datapoints required for meaningful median analysis.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $44M (low) to $60M (high), with a median point of $52M. Key uncertainties include the limited applicability of peer multiples given the nascent and heterogeneous nature of the quantum computing landscape, and the absence of revenue or unit economics data to validate the proxy. The range reflects reliance on a single method; expansion of this analysis would benefit from revenue-based multiples or venture-stage benchmarking if such data becomes available.
